events.preview: Feb 2 resolved seven questions. The League Cup update was correct; Saif al-Islam and Zintan were included but underweighted, while Myanmar panel, Harden trade, Brazil crash, and Tehran market fire were outside the active

blocks.text: Feb 2 resolved seven questions. The League Cup update was correct; Saif al-Islam and Zintan were included but underweighted, while Myanmar panel, Harden trade, Brazil crash, and Tehran market fire were outside the active sets. I’m scanning Feb 2 evidence now for the Feb 3 and nearby questions.

input.command:
/usr/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import json, os
path='articles/2026/02/02/articles.jsonl'
print('exists', os.path.exists(path))
if not os.path.exists(path): raise SystemExit
lines=0; usable=0; bydate={}
for line in open(path):
    lines+=1
    a=json.loads(line); dp=a.get('date_publish') or ''
    if dp <= '2026-02-02': usable+=1
    bydate[dp]=bydate.get(dp,0)+1
print('lines',lines,'usable<=Feb2',usable,'latest_dates', sorted(bydate)[-10:])
checks={
'336425 Ukraine market shelling': lambda t: ('market shelling' in t) or ('market' in t and ('shelling' in t or 'strike' in t) and ('ukraine' in t or 'ukrainian' in t or 'kostiantynivka' in t or 'kupiansk' in t or 'donetsk' in t or 'pokrovsk' in t)),
'426743 Saif burial': lambda t: 'saif al-islam' in t and ('buried' in t or 'burial' in t or 'funeral' in t or 'killed' in t or 'death' in t),
'57193 SCOTUS map': lambda t: ('supreme court' in t and 'congressional map' in t) or ('california' in t and 'congressional map' in t),
'579252 Saleh city': lambda t: 'saleh mohammadi' in t or ('wrestler' in t and 'public execution' in t),
'630740 Lebanon collapse': lambda t: ('leban' in t and ('building collapse' in t or 'residential building' in t) and ('15' in t or 'dead' in t or 'killed' in t)),
'883856 Moscow officer': lambda t: ('shot in moscow' in t) or ('senior military officer' in t and 'moscow' in t) or 'yaroslav moskalik' in t,
'14689 school shooting country': lambda t: ('secondary school' in t and 'shooting' in t) or ('school shooting' in t and ('germany' in t or 'sweden' in t or 'canada' in t or 'france' in t or 'austria' in t)),
'739916 carrier': lambda t: ('uss abraham lincoln' in t and ('carrier' in t or 'strike group' in t)) or ('aircraft carrier' in t and 'middle east' in t),
'198725 Narges exile': lambda t: 'narges mohammadi' in t and ('exile' in t or 'sentenced' in t or 'zanjan' in t or 'qazvin' in t),
'534224 downhill women': lambda t: ('women' in t and 'downhill' in t and ('olympic' in t or 'milan-cortina' in t or 'cortina' in t)) or ('vonn' in t and 'olympic' in t),
'312764 Egypt planning': lambda t: ('egypt' in t and 'planning minister' in t) or ('minister of planning' in t and 'egypt' in t),
'527803 Canada school town': lambda t: ('school shooting' in t and 'canada' in t) or ('la loche' in t and 'shooting' in t),
'795774 French activist': lambda t: ('far-right activist' in t and 'france' in t) or ('macron' in t and 'meloni' in t and ('killed' in t or 'activist' in t)),
'456284 WaPo publisher': lambda t: ('washington post' in t and 'will lewis' in t) or ('publisher' in t and 'washington post' in t),
'318661 Colombia peace': lambda t: ('colombia' in t and 'peace talks' in t) or ('eln' in t and 'peace talks' in t),
'398988 Lebanon abduction': lambda t: ('israel' in t and 'abducted' in t and 'lebanon' in t) or ('southern lebanon' in t and 'official' in t and 'abducted' in t),
'487245 address tension': lambda t: ('address' in t and 'president' in t and ('iran' in t or 'venezuela' in t or 'china' in t or 'russia' in t)),
'745180 Cyclone Gezani': lambda t: 'gezani' in t or ('madagascar' in t and 'cyclone' in t),
'613203 Tractor AFC': lambda t: ('tractor' in t and ('afc' in t or 'champions league' in t or 'playoff' in t)),
'180958 Super Bowl': lambda t: ('super bowl' in t and ('seahawks' in t or 'patriots' in t or 'seattle' in t or 'new england' in t)),
'538697 Gaza troops': lambda t: ('8000 troops' in t or '8,000 troops' in t or ('troops' in t and 'gaza' in t and ('ready' in t or 'june' in t))),
'304287 Bangladesh opposition': lambda t: ('bangladesh' in t and ('opposition' in t or 'election' in t or 'parliament' in t)) or 'jamaat-e-islami' in t,
}
arts=[]
for line in open(path):
    a=json.loads(line)
    if (a.get('date_publish') or '')>'2026-02-02': continue
    title=a.get('title') or ''; c=a.get('content') or ''
    t=(title+'\\n'+c).lower()
    arts.append((a,title,c,t))
for label,fn in checks.items():
    hits=[]
    for a,title,c,t in arts:
        if fn(t):
            idx=0
            for kw in ['market','saif','supreme court','saleh','building collapse','moscow','school shooting','abraham lincoln','narges','downhill','planning minister','canada','far-right','washington post','peace talks','abducted','address','gezani','tractor','super bowl','gaza','bangladesh']:
                j=t.find(kw)
                if j"'!=-1:
                    idx=j; break
            snippet=(title+'"' | '+c[max(0,idx-350):idx+1300]).replace('\\n',' ')
            hits.append((a.get('date_publish'),a.get('source'),snippet[:1800]))
            if len(hits)>=7: break
    print('\\n##',label,'hits',len(hits))
    for dp,src,sn in hits:
        print('-',dp,src,sn)
PY"

And importantly, there have been economic consequences for Israel and some of those doing business in it – echoing the boycott movement against apartheid South Africa in the 1980s. Companies such as the French supermarket chain Carrefour were forced to close a number of outlets in the Middle East amid public anger over its links to Israeli firms operating in illegal settlements in the occupied West Bank. Other companies associated with Israel’s actions, including Airbnb – which allows Israelis in illegal settlements to rent their properties – and Microsoft, whose Azure cloud services have supported the Israeli military, have all faced internal dissent and reputational damage because of their ties to the Israeli government. As a consequence of public pressure, pension funds from around the world, including Spain, Norway, Denmark, France and Ireland, have divested from Israeli assets linked to settlements, or withdrawn investments from companies tied to Israel. “BDS and boycotts have changed Israel’s global trade landscape,” Avi Balashnikov, chairman of the Israel Export Institute, conceded in September 2024. “We fight every day, hour by hour, for Israeli industry abroad,” he added. “Economic boycotts and BDS organisations present major challenges, and in some countries, we are forced to operate under the radar.” Moving spotlight Despite a US-brokered “ceasefire“, Israeli attacks on Gaza have killed more than 500 people since the agreement was reached in October, including at least 31 on Saturday. The comments from the 86-year-old Ayatollah Ali Khamenei are the most-direct threat he’s made so far as the aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln and associated American warships are in the Arabian Sea, sent by Trump there after Tehran's bloody crackdown on nationwide protests. It remains unclear whether Trump will use force. He's repeatedly said Iran wants to negotiate and has brought up Tehran's nuclear program as another issue he wants to see resolved. But Khamenei also referred to the nationwide protests as “a coup,” hardening the government's position as tens of thousands of people reportedly have been detained since the start of the demonstrations. Sedition charges in Iran can carry the death penalty, which again renews concerns about Tehran carrying out mass executions for those arrested — a red line for Trump. Iran had also planned a live-fire military drill for Sunday and Monday in the strategic Strait of Hormuz, the narrow mouth of the Persian Gulf through which a fifth of all oil traded passes. The US military's Central Command had warned against threatening American warships or aircraft during the drill or disrupting commercial traffic. Khamenei spoke to a crowd at his compound in Tehran as Iran marked the start of a dayslong commemoration of the country's 1979 Islamic Revolution. But Mr Baqaei said Tehran “never accepts ultimatums” and that he could not confirm any such message was received. Regional players have pushed for diplomacy to defuse tensions. Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi was in Turkey last week, and held further calls with his Egyptian, Saudi and Turkish counterparts, he said on messaging platform Telegram. “President Trump said no nuclear weapons, and we fully agree. We fully agree with that. That could be a very good deal,” Mr Araghchi told news outlet CNN on Feb 1. “Of course, in return, we expect sanctions lifting. So, that deal is possible. Let’s not talk about impossible things.” Mr Baqaei said the rallying of neighbouring states around a diplomatic solution demonstrated a fear that any US attack would drag the region into conflict, echoing Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who warned on Feb 1 that a US attack would trigger a “regional war”. - 2026-02-02 www.independent.co.uk Oscar-nominated screenwriter arrested in Iran for criticising brutal crackdown on protesters |  has relied on mass killings to ensure its survival while ignoring the basic and inalienable rights of citizens — including the fundamental right to seek change in the political system,” the letter read. Among the signatories are Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi and winner of the Sakharov Prize for Freedom of Thought, Nasrin Sotoudeh. Others include prominent lawyers, authors and activists. Mr Mahmoudian’s co-writer Jafar Panahi also signed the but he remains in exile outside of the country. “I met Mehdi Mahmoudian in prison,” Mr Panahi said in a statement released after Mahmoudian’s arrest. “From the very first days, he stood out — not only because of his calm demeanor and kind conduct but also because of a rare sense of responsibility toward others. “Whenever a new prisoner arrived, Mehdi would try to provide them with basic necessities and, more importantly, offer reassurance. He became a quiet pillar inside the prison—someone inmates of all beliefs and backgrounds trusted and confided in.” Mr Panahi recalled the last time he spoke to his friend: “Forty-eight hours before his arrest, we spoke on the phone and then exchanged a few messages. I sent him my last message at four in the morning. “By noon the next day, there was no reply. I grew worried and contacted mutual friends; none of them had heard from him. A few hours later, BBC Persian officially announced that Mehdi Mahmoudian, along with Abdollah Momeni and Vida Rabbani, had been arrested.”

for q in queries:
    hits=[]
    for path in sorted(glob.glob('articles/2026/0[12]/*/articles.jsonl')):
        for line in open(path):
            a=json.loads(line)
            if (a.get('date_publish') or '')>'2026-02-02': continue
            text=((a.get('title') or '')+'\\n'+(a.get('content') or '')).lower()
            if all(w.lower() in text for w in q.split()):

        if len(hits)>=8: break
    print('\\n',q,'=>')
    for h in hits: print(h)

/usr/bin/bash -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import csv,json,os
from collections import Counter
print('state_exists', os.path.exists('state.json'))
if os.path.exists('state.json'):
    print(json.dumps(json.load(open('state.json')), indent=2)[:5000])
rows=list(csv.DictReader(open('market.csv', newline='')))
active=[r for r in rows if r['is_resolved'].lower()=='false']
resolved=[r for r in rows if r['is_resolved'].lower()=='true']
print('rows',len(rows),'active',len(active),'resolved',len(resolved))
print('active_without_prediction',sum(1 for r in active if not r['my_prediction'] or r['my_prediction']=='None'))
print('active_pred_dates',Counter(r['my_prediction_date'] for r in active).most_common(30))
print('\\nResolved on/after 2026-02-02:')
for r in resolved:
    if r['resolution_date']>='2026-02-02':
        print('\\nQ',r['qid'],'resdate',r['resolution_date'],'GT',r['ground_truth'])
        print('title:',r['title'])
        print('my:',r['my_prediction'],'date',r['my_prediction_date'])
print('\\nActive upcoming <=2026-02-12:')
for r in active:
    if r['resolution_date'] <= '2026-02-12':
        print('\\nQ',r['qid'],'resdate',r['resolution_date'])
        print('title:',r['title'])
        print('my:',r['my_prediction'],'date',r['my_prediction_date'])
PY"
